purpose: Provides precise angular position feedback for spindle orientation control and tool positioning accuracy

inputs.name: orientation_stop_position
inputs.description: Target position for spindle orientation in encoder pulses

outputs.name: position_error
outputs.description: Difference between actual and commanded position

outputs.name: orientation_time
outputs.description: Time required to complete orientation movement

outputs.name: spindle_sequence_state
outputs.description: Current state in spindle orientation sequence

outputs.name: orientation_completed
outputs.description: Boolean indicating successful completion of orientation

states.name: signal_degradation
states.description: Position coder signal quality degradation from electrical disconnection and wear

states.name: one_rotation_signal_degradation
states.description: One-rotation reference signal degradation from detection circuit wear

states.name: signal_integrity_degradation
states.description: Overall signal integrity degradation from noise and interference

events.name: position_coder_disconnected
events.description: Position coder disconnected due to severe signal degradation

events.name: one_rotation_signal_detection_error
events.description: Position coder one-rotation signal detection error

events.name: one_rotation_signal_not_detected
events.description: Position coder one-rotation signal not detected

events.name: position_coder_signal_error
events.description: Position coder signal error due to integrity degradation

parameters.name: one_rotation_degradation_timescale
parameters.description: Time constant for one-rotation signal degradation

parameters.name: integrity_degradation_timescale
parameters.description: Time constant for signal integrity degradation

parameters.name: signal_stress_factor
parameters.description: Stress factor for main signal degradation

parameters.name: rotation_stress_factor
parameters.description: Stress factor for rotation signal degradation

parameters.name: environmental_stress_factor
parameters.description: Environmental stress factor for signal integrity

parameters.name: error_amplification_factor
parameters.description: Factor by which degradation amplifies errors

parameters.name: time_penalty_factor
parameters.description: Factor for orientation time penalty due to degradation

parameters.name: rotation_error_threshold
parameters.description: One-rotation degradation threshold for detection error

parameters.name: rotation_failure_threshold
parameters.description: One-rotation degradation threshold for signal loss

state_transition_logic: signal_degradation decreases by (1.0 / signal_degradation_timescale) * signal_stress_factor * dt, clamped to [0.0, 1.0]
one_rotation_signal_degradation decreases by (1.0 / one_rotation_degradation_timescale) * rotation_stress_factor * dt, clamped to [0.0, 1.0]
signal_integrity_degradation decreases by (1.0 / integrity_degradation_timescale) * environmental_stress_factor * dt, clamped to [0.0, 1.0]
output_logic: spindle_sequence_state = 'a' if signal_degradation > 0.8 else ('b' if signal_degradation > 0.6 else ('c' if signal_degradation > 0.4 else ('d' if signal_degradation > 0.2 else 'e')))
orientation_completed = signal_degradation > disconnection_threshold and one_rotation_signal_degradation > rotation_failure_threshold
event_logic: one_rotation_signal_detection_error occurs when one_rotation_signal_degradation <= rotation_error_threshold and one_rotation_signal_degradation > rotation_failure_threshold
assumptions: Signal degradation is primarily driven by electrical connection wear and environmental factors
One-rotation signal degradation is independent of main position signal degradation
Position errors increase proportionally with signal quality degradation
Orientation time increases as signal quality degrades due to retry attempts
All degradation processes follow exponential decay patterns
Environmental stress factors remain relatively constant during operation
